name: DAK Dashboard
description: Self-hosted wall dashboard for Raspberry Pi — iCloud photos, merged ICS calendars, weather forecast, clock, Home Assistant sensors, served by FastAPI with a TypeScript/esbuild frontend in Chromium kiosk mode.

userStories.title: Project Scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a clean project structure with all config and build files so that the rest of the implementation can proceed without setup friction.
acceptanceCriteria: Directories exist: sources/, tests/, src/modules/, static/js/, static/css/, systemd/, docs/superpowers/plans/, docs/superpowers/specs/, tasks/
sources/__init__.py exists (empty)
requirements.txt contains pinned versions: fastapi==0.111.0, uvicorn[standard]==0.29.0, httpx==0.27.0, icalendar==5.0.12, python-dateutil==2.9.0, respx==0.21.1, pytest==8.2.0, pytest-asyncio==0.23.7
package.json has build script: esbuild src/app.ts --bundle --outfile=static/js/app.js --minify and watch script with --watch flag
package.json devDependencies includes esbuild ^0.20.0 and typescript ^5.4.0
tsconfig.json exists with target ES2020, strict true, noEmit true, lib [ES2020, DOM]

config.example.json has all keys: icloud.shareToken, icloud.photoIntervalSeconds, calendars[] (name/url/color), weather (provider/latitude/longitude/accuweatherApiKey), homeAssistant (url/token/entities[]), display (calendarDaysAhead/weatherDays)
config.json exists (copied from example) and listed in .gitignore
.gitignore excludes .venv/, __pycache__/, *.pyc, node_modules/, static/js/app.js
Python venv created at .venv/ and pip install -r requirements.txt exits 0
npm install exits 0
All scaffold files committed to git
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pydantic Response Models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed Pydantic models for all API response shapes so that the FastAPI endpoint is type-safe and the frontend data contract is explicit.
acceptanceCriteria: models.py exists importing BaseModel from pydantic and datetime from datetime
Photo model has fields: id: str, url: str
CalendarEvent model has fields: id: str, title: str, start: datetime, end: datetime, all_day: bool, calendar_name: str, color: str
WeatherDay model has fields: date: str, icon: str, temp_high: float, temp_low: float, precip_percent: int
HaEntity model has fields: id: str, label: str, state: str, unit: str
DashboardData model has fields: photos: list[Photo], events: list[CalendarEvent], weather: list[WeatherDay], ha_entities: list[HaEntity], photo_interval_seconds: int, server_time: datetime
python3 -c "from models import DashboardData; print('models OK')" prints 'models OK' with exit code 0
Committed to git with message: feat: add Pydantic response models
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Config Loading
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed config loader that reads config.json and exposes all values as Python dataclasses so that sources access config via typed attributes rather than raw dict keys.
acceptanceCriteria: config.py contains dataclasses: ICloudConfig, CalendarConfig, WeatherConfig, HaEntityConfig, HomeAssistantConfig, DisplayConfig, AppConfig
ICloudConfig has share_token: str and photo_interval_seconds: int (mapped from JSON camelCase shareToken)
WeatherConfig has provider: str, latitude: float, longitude: float, accuweather_api_key: str
HomeAssistantConfig has url: str, token: str, entities: list[HaEntityConfig]
DisplayConfig has calendar_days_ahead: int and weather_days: int (mapped from calendarDaysAhead/weatherDays)
load_config(path: str = 'config.json') -> AppConfig function exists and maps all camelCase JSON keys to snake_case fields
tests/conftest.py exists with sample_config fixture that writes full config JSON to tmp_path and returns path string

tests/test_config.py has test_load_config_returns_typed_object asserting cfg.icloud.share_token, cfg.icloud.photo_interval_seconds, cfg.calendars[0].name, cfg.weather.provider, cfg.display.calendar_days_ahead
pytest tests/test_config.py -v passes (1 test)
Committed to git with message: feat: add config loader
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: In-Memory TTL Cache
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a simple in-memory cache with per-key TTLs and stale-value fallback so that /api/data never blocks on an external API call.
acceptanceCriteria: cache.py contains Cache class with set(key: str, value: Any, ttl_seconds: int) -> None method
Cache.get(key: str, return_stale: bool = False) -> Any: returns None for missing, None for expired (unless return_stale=True), returns value for valid keys
Cache.is_expired(key: str) -> bool: returns True for missing or expired keys
Internal storage is dict[str, tuple[Any, float]] using time.monotonic() for expiry
tests/test_cache.py has 5 tests: test_set_and_get, test_get_missing_returns_none, test_expired_returns_none (TTL=0 sleep 10ms), test_stale_returns_last_value_when_flagged, test_overwrite
pytest tests/test_cache.py -v passes (5 tests)
Committed to git with message: feat: in-memory TTL cache
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: FastAPI Skeleton with Mock /api/data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a running FastAPI server that serves static/index.html and returns a valid empty /api/data response so that the frontend can be built against a stable contract before real data sources are wired.
acceptanceCriteria: main.py has create_app(config_path: str = 'config.json') -> FastAPI factory function
main.py has module-level app = create_app() for uvicorn
GET /api/data returns HTTP 200 with DashboardData JSON: photos:[], events:[], weather:[], ha_entities:[], photo_interval_seconds from config, server_time as UTC ISO string
GET / and any unknown path serves static/index.html via FileResponse
StaticFiles mounted at /static/css and /static/js
pytest.ini exists with [pytest] section and asyncio_mode = auto
tests/test_api.py has test_api_data_returns_200 (async, ASGITransport, asserts status 200 and all required JSON keys) and test_static_index_served (asserts GET / returns 200)
static/index.html exists (placeholder)
pytest tests/test_api.py -v passes (2 tests)
python3 main.py starts without errors; curl http://localhost:3000/api/data returns valid JSON

userStories.title: iCloud Shared Album Photo Source
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the backend to fetch photos from an iCloud shared album via its undocumented JSON API and proxy them so that the frontend displays photos without CORS issues.
acceptanceCriteria: sources/icloud.py has async def get_photos(cfg: AppConfig) -> list[Photo]
get_photos POSTs {streamCtag: None} to https://p00-sharedstreams.icloud.com/{token}/sharedstreams/webstream with Content-Type: application/json
If response has X-Apple-MMe-Host header, request is repeated using that host as base URL
get_photos POSTs photo guids to .../sharedstreams/webasseturls to get download URLs
Returns list[Photo] where Photo.url = /api/photo/{guid} (not raw iCloud URL)
Module-level _photo_url_map dict[str, str] stores guid to real iCloud URL; populated on each call
get_photo_url(photo_id: str) -> str | None function exported from module
main.py GET /api/photo/{photo_id} uses get_photo_url and proxies bytes via StreamingResponse with correct content-type; returns HTTP 404 if guid unknown
tests/test_icloud.py has test_get_photos_returns_photo_list (respx mock, asserts 2 photos, Photo.id=='guid1', Photo.url=='/api/photo/guid1') and test_get_photos_handles_empty_album (asserts [])
pytest tests/test_icloud.py -v passes (2 tests)
Committed to git with message: feat: iCloud shared album photo source
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Calendar ICS Source with Recurring Events
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the backend to fetch multiple ICS calendar feeds concurrently, parse all event types including recurring ones, and return a single merged sorted list so that the frontend shows a unified multi-calendar view.
acceptanceCriteria: sources/calendar.py has async def get_events(cfg: AppConfig) -> list[CalendarEvent]
All ICS URLs fetched concurrently via httpx.AsyncClient (not sequentially)
Events parsed with icalendar.Calendar.from_ical(); only VEVENT components processed
All-day events: CalendarEvent.all_day = True when DTSTART.dt is datetime.date not datetime.datetime
Recurring events with RRULE expanded using dateutil.rrule.rrulestr; EXDATE exclusions applied
Events filtered to window: today 00:00:00 UTC through today + calendarDaysAhead + 1 days
Result sorted: primary=event.start.date(), secondary=not all_day (all-day first), tertiary=event.start
Each event color assigned from CalendarConfig.color of matching calendar
If any ICS fetch fails, that calendar is silently skipped; others still processed
tests/fixtures/simple.ics has one timed event (Trh Dka) and one all-day event (Celodení akce)
tests/fixtures/recurring.ics has a daily recurring Standup event
tests/test_calendar.py has 4 respx-mocked tests: test_parses_simple_events, test_all_day_events_flagged, test_recurring_events_expanded, test_calendar_color_assigned
pytest tests/test_calendar.py -v passes (4 tests)
Committed to git with message: feat: ICS calendar source with recurring event support
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Weather Source — Open-Meteo and AccuWeather Providers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a weather provider abstraction with Open-Meteo as the default (no API key) and AccuWeather as a configurable alternative so that the weather widget shows normalized forecasts regardless of provider.
acceptanceCriteria: sources/weather.py defines ICON_KEYS: dict[int, str] mapping WMO codes to: 0-1=sunny, 2=partly-cloudy, 3=cloudy, 45/48=fog, 51-57=rainy, 61-64=rainy, 65=heavy-rain, 66-67=rainy/heavy-rain, 71-77=snow, 80-81=rainy, 82=heavy-rain, 85-86=snow, 95/96/99=storm
WeatherProvider abstract base class with async def get_forecast(cfg: AppConfig) -> list[WeatherDay]

OpenMeteoProvider calls https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast with params: latitude, longitude, daily=weathercode,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_probability_max, timezone=auto, forecast_days=cfg.display.weather_days
AccuWeatherProvider does two-step: geoposition search for location key, then 5-day daily forecast endpoint
Both providers return WeatherDay with date (YYYY-MM-DD), icon (normalized key), temp_high, temp_low, precip_percent
get_forecast(cfg) dispatches to OpenMeteoProvider when cfg.weather.provider='openmeteo', AccuWeatherProvider when 'accuweather'
_AW_ICON_KEYS dict maps AccuWeather icon IDs 1-44 to normalized keys
test_openmeteo_returns_5_days: mocks Open-Meteo response, asserts 5 WeatherDay objects
test_openmeteo_normalizes_icons: asserts WMO 0=sunny, 61=rainy, 71=snow, 95=storm, 45=fog
test_openmeteo_temps_and_precip: asserts temp_high, temp_low, precip_percent correct values
test_all_icon_keys_are_valid: all ICON_KEYS values are in {sunny,partly-cloudy,cloudy,rainy,heavy-rain,snow,storm,fog}
pytest tests/test_weather.py -v passes (4 tests)
Committed to git with message: feat: weather source with Open-Meteo and AccuWeather providers
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Home Assistant Entity Source
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the backend to concurrently fetch the state of configured Home Assistant entities so that sensor values appear on the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: sources/homeassistant.py has async def get_entities(cfg: AppConfig) -> list[HaEntity]
Returns [] immediately if cfg.home_assistant.entities is empty (no HTTP calls made)
All entities fetched concurrently via asyncio.gather()
Each entity fetched via GET {ha_url}/api/states/{entity_id} with Authorization: Bearer {token} header
HaEntity built from response: id=entity_id, label=HaEntityConfig.label (from config), state=data['state'], unit=data['attributes']['unit_of_measurement'] (empty string if missing)
Failed individual entity fetches (non-200, timeout, error) are omitted; other entities still returned
test_fetches_entity_state: respx mock, asserts state=='22.5', unit=='°C', label=='Obývák'
test_returns_empty_when_no_entities_configured: sets entities=[], asserts []
test_skips_unreachable_entity: respx 404 mock, asserts []
pytest tests/test_homeassistant.py -v passes (3 tests)
Committed to git with message: feat: Home Assistant entity source
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire All Sources into /api/data with Background Cache
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the /api/data endpoint to return real data from all four sources, fetched eagerly on startup and refreshed in the background, so that every response is instant and never depends on an in-flight external call.
acceptanceCriteria: main.py create_app() uses load_config(config_path) and creates Cache instance per app
On startup event: all four sources fetched concurrently via asyncio.gather() with TTLs: photos=3600s, events=300s, weather=1800s, ha_entities=60s
Background asyncio.create_task loops started for each source: sleep(ttl), re-fetch, update cache; stale value kept on error
GET /api/data reads all keys with return_stale=True; never makes external calls; always returns within 50ms

tests/test_api_integration.py has test_api_data_returns_real_data: mocks Open-Meteo, iCloud webstream+webasseturls, both ICS feeds, HA API; calls startup; asserts weather has 5 items, weather[0].icon=='sunny', photos has 1 item, photo_interval_seconds==30
pytest -v (all tests) passes
curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/data | python3 -m json.tool shows valid JSON with all 6 top-level fields
Committed to git with message: feat: wire all sources into /api/data with background TTL cache
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: TypeScript Frontend Scaffold, Types, and HTML Shell

acceptanceCriteria: src/types.ts exports interfaces Photo (id, url), CalendarEvent (id, title, start, end, all_day, calendar_name, color), WeatherDay (date, icon, temp_high, temp_low, precip_percent), HaEntity (id, label, state, unit), DashboardData (photos, events, weather, ha_entities, photo_interval_seconds, server_time) — field names match Python models exactly
src/api.ts exports fetchData(): Promise<DashboardData> that fetches /api/data and throws on non-OK response
static/index.html has link tags for base.css, photo.css, calendar.css, clock.css, weather.css, mini-calendar.css and script tag for /static/js/app.js
static/index.html has containers: #photo-area (with #photo-a and #photo-b img children), #calendar-area, #clock-area, #weather-area, #mini-cal-area, #ha-area
static/css/base.css defines .dashboard CSS grid: 2 columns, 5 rows, grid-template-areas with photo spanning full width (55vh), calendar left rows 2-5, clock/weather/mini-cal/ha in right column
.ha-area has grid-area: ha and display: none by default
src/app.ts minimal entry point that calls fetchData() on load; compiles without error

userStories.title: Clock Module
userStories.description: As a user, I want a large digital clock in the top-right area updating every second so that I can always see the current time without any network call.
acceptanceCriteria: src/modules/clock.ts exports startClock(container: HTMLElement): void

userStories.title: Photo Crossfade Rotation Module
userStories.description: As a user, I want the background photo to smoothly rotate through iCloud photos every N seconds with a 1-second crossfade so that there are no blank frames or jarring cuts between photos.
acceptanceCriteria: src/modules/photo.ts exports render(data: Photo[], container: HTMLElement, photoIntervalSeconds: number): void
Uses two stacked img elements (#photo-a and #photo-b); only one has photo-img--active class at any time
On rotation: non-active img src set to next URL; on onload, photo-img--active class swapped — CSS opacity transition handles the fade
First render sets initial photo on #photo-a immediately (no wait for interval)
Rotation interval timer reset when photoIntervalSeconds changes between calls
Photo order randomized on first call when photos array changes
static/css/photo.css: both .photo-img have position absolute, inset 0, width/height 100%, object-fit cover, opacity 0, transition opacity 1s ease-in-out; .photo-img--active has opacity 1
src/app.ts updated to import renderPhotos and call renderPhotos(data.photos, document.getElementById('photo-area')!, data.photo_interval_seconds)

userStories.title: systemd Service Files
userStories.description: As a developer, I need systemd service files for the FastAPI server and Chromium kiosk browser so that both auto-start on RPi boot and restart on crash.
acceptanceCriteria: systemd/dak-server.service has [Unit] After=network.target Wants=network.target; [Service] User=ubuntu, WorkingDirectory=/home/ubuntu/dak, ExecStart=/home/ubuntu/dak/.venv/bin/uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 3000, Restart=always, RestartSec=5, StandardOutput=journal, StandardError=journal; [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target

systemd/dak-browser.service has [Unit] After=dak-server.service graphical-session.target Wants=graphical-session.target; [Service] User=ubuntu, DISPLAY=:0, XAUTHORITY=/home/ubuntu/.Xauthority, ExecStartPre=/bin/sleep 3, ExecStart=/usr/bin/chromium-browser with flags --kiosk --noerrdialogs --disable-infobars --no-first-run --check-for-update-interval=31536000 --disable-translate --disable-features=Translate http://localhost:3000, Restart=always, RestartSec=5; [Install] WantedBy=graphical-session.target
Both files have a comment noting ubuntu should be replaced with pi on Raspberry Pi OS
Committed to git with message: feat: systemd service files for server and kiosk browser
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: README and Deployment Guide
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a complete README covering Ubuntu Server setup, dependency installation, frontend build, systemd enablement, and config documentation so that the dashboard can be deployed on a fresh RPi from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md has Requirements section: RPi + Ubuntu Server 22.04+, Python 3.11+, Node.js 18+, Chromium, unclutter
Setup Step 1 — system deps: sudo apt install -y chromium-browser unclutter xorg lightdm
Setup Step 2 — clone and configure: git clone, cp config.example.json config.json, edit config
Setup Step 3 — Python deps: python3 -m venv .venv, source .venv/bin/activate, pip install -r requirements.txt
Setup Step 4 — build frontend: npm install && npm run build
Setup Step 5 — systemd: sudo cp systemd/*.service /etc/systemd/system/, daemon-reload, enable + start both services
Setup Step 6 — auto-login: set graphical.target as default, configure lightdm.conf autologin-user=ubuntu
Updating config section: edit config.json then sudo systemctl restart dak-server (no rebuild)
Updating frontend section: npm run build then sudo systemctl restart dak-server
Calendar ICS URLs section: how to get ICS links from iCloud, Google, MS365/Outlook
iCloud Share Token section: how to extract token from share URL
Logs section: journalctl -u dak-server -f and journalctl -u dak-browser -f
Committed to git with message: docs: deployment guide and setup README
Typecheck passes
